features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: read stun msg id safely from possbily misaligned buffer.

patches.author: Anna Weine
patches.subject: Implement RTCCertificateStats && RTCTransport certificate ids

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: implement candidate-pair packetsSent/packetsReceived

features.summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's connection transport area enhance its handling of real-time communications. Notably, improvements were made to safely read STUN (Session Traversal Utilities for NAT) messages, ensuring that data is accurately processed even if it's improperly aligned in memory. Additionally, new statistics related to RTCCertificates—used for secure connections in WebRTC (the technology behind video calls)—were introduced, including details on certificate IDs, which help in managing security. Furthermore, the implementation of candidate-pair packet statistics allows for better monitoring of network performance during calls by tracking packets sent and received, ultimately contributing to a more reliable user experience in real-time communications. **This emphasis on security and performance boosts the reliability of web-based video calls and streaming, which is essential in today's digital interactions.**

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alexandra Borovova
patches.subject: Invalidate device cache when recording a browser window on desktop in the privileged context.

patches.author: Andreas Pehrson
patches.subject: Add gtest.

features.summary: Recent updates to the camera and audio capture features in Firefox aim to enhance privacy and functionality when users record browser windows on desktop. By ensuring that the device cache is invalidated during such recordings in a privileged context, Firefox enhances user security by preventing unauthorized access to sensitive media device information. This change is important for maintaining user trust, especially as more people engage in online meetings and live streams, where secure and reliable media interactions are essential. _Additionally, the introduction of testing (gtest) will help ensure that these features operate smoothly and reliably in future updates._

features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Activate clang-tidy's modernize-type-traits check

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: implement webrtc outbound-rtp keyFramesEncoded stats

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, specifically the RTCPeerConnection component, introduced enhancements that help improve video and audio performance during real-time communications. One significant change is the implementation of new statistics for encoded keyframes in WebRTC sessions, which allows developers to better analyze and optimize video quality during calls. Additionally, by incorporating modern code checks using clang-tidy, the codebase is being refined to follow best practices, which can lead to smoother functionality and easier maintenance in the long run. These improvements are essential as they help ensure WebRTC continues to deliver high-quality and reliable video calling experiences.

features.label: libwebrtc integration

patches.author: az
patches.subject: Parse VP8/VP9 quantization parameters in the WebRTC platform decoder for the qpSum stat

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum
patches.subject: Bug 2047265 (MOZ) - switch from deprecated OnDroppedFrame to OnFrameDropped

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc integration in Firefox focus on improving video performance and reporting. By parsing VP8 and VP9 quantization parameters, the platform decoder can now accurately track video quality through the qpSum statistic, which provides insights into compression efficiency. Additionally, the transition from the deprecated OnDroppedFrame method to the more current OnFrameDropped helps ensure that the browser handles frame drops more effectively during video calls, enhancing the overall user experience. **This means users will likely notice improved video quality and smoother playback during WebRTC sessions**.

features.label: Negotiation engine (JSEP)

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Parse and properly use Opus ptime/minptime/maxptime from in sipcc SDP parser

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox's negotiation engine, specifically the JSEP (JavaScript Session Establishment Protocol), enhance its handling of audio in video calls using the Opus codec, a popular choice for its quality and efficiency. The patches ensure that the parameters related to packet time (ptime), minimum packet time (minptime), and maximum packet time (maxptime) are accurately parsed and applied from the SIPCC (Session Initiation Protocol Communications Client) SDP (Session Description Protocol) parser. This improvement matters because it enables better control over audio stream qualities, resulting in clearer calls and reduced latency, ultimately leading to an enhanced user experience in real-time communication.

features.label: Objc Video Capture

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Comply with clang-tidy's modernize-use-nullptr check for dom/

features.summary: Recent updates to the video capture functionality in Firefox have focused on improving code quality by complying with modern coding standards. Specifically, the implementation has adopted the use of `nullptr` instead of traditional null values, following clang-tidy’s guidelines. This change enhances the robustness of the codebase, making it easier to maintain while ensuring compatibility with contemporary C++ development practices. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this type of improvement plays a vital role in maintaining a reliable and effective video capture experience.

features.label: Video Engine

patches.author: Alexandra Borovova
patches.subject: Allow capturing a browser in a priviliged context beyond Nighly.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox video engine have improved the ability to capture video from the browser in more secure or privileged contexts, extending this capability beyond just the Nightly version. This change is significant because it enhances the potential for applications that require video capture, such as screen recording tools or certain interactive web experiences. With this advancement, developers can more easily create features that utilize live video feeds directly from the browser, leading to richer user interactions and more innovative web applications. **This enhancement ultimately makes it easier for users to share and interact with video content securely.**

features.label: WebRTC

patches.author: Philipp Hancke
patches.subject: add IPC serialization for keyFramesEncoded stats

features.summary: Recent updates in WebRTC, which is the technology enabling real-time audio and video communication in web browsers, have introduced improvements in how keyframe encoding statistics are handled. By adding IPC (Inter-Process Communication) serialization for keyFramesEncoded stats, Firefox can now more efficiently gather and report information on video encoding performance. This change provides developers with better insights into video quality metrics, ultimately contributing to **enhanced user experiences during video calls and online streaming**.

features.summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's connection transport area have introduced several improvements focused on enhancing the reliability and tracking of WebRTC (the technology behind video calls). Notably, **implementing RTCTransportStats** allows for detailed insights on transportation security, including the TLS version and cipher used for encrypted communications, which is vital for user privacy and data integrity. Additionally, changes to how data is read from potentially misaligned buffers ensure that communication remains stable, even in less-than-ideal network conditions. These enhancements not only bolster the performance of real-time communications but also support better diagnostics and analytics for developers working with WebRTC applications.

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which powers technologies like video calls and real-time communications, include several enhancements to its functionality and performance. Notably, **the implementation of outbound-rtp keyFramesEncoded stats** allows developers to better track video quality by monitoring the number of keyframes encoded, which are crucial for maintaining video clarity during playback. Additionally, the addition of `getRemoteCertificates` enhances security by allowing the retrieval of remote certificates used in sessions, ensuring that communications remain encrypted and secure. Other updates improve the internal handling of statistics and memory management, streamlining how the application allocates resources, which contributes to a more efficient and reliable communication experience.

features.summary: Recent changes to Firefox's libwebrtc integration focus on enhancing video and audio handling capabilities in real-time communications. Key improvements include the ability to effectively parse VP8 and VP9 quantization parameters for better video quality assessment, and a cleanup of deprecated functions, which ensures the WebRTC platform remains current and efficient. Furthermore, there is a shift towards safer memory management practices, as seen in the adoption of `std::unique_ptr` for various decoder and encoder creations. Enhancements to the handling of H.264 video standards and better accuracy in assessing media capabilities based on resolution and framerate are also significant. Together, these updates streamline performance and improve the reliability of video calls, making **real-time communication clearer and more efficient for users**.

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox's negotiation engine, which supports WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), have focused on enhancing the handling of audio codecs, specifically the Opus codec. Changes include improvements to how Opus parameters such as `ptime`, `minptime`, and `maxptime` are parsed and utilized from the SDP (Session Description Protocol), which is essential for setting up media sessions. Additionally, there have been enhancements in memory management by migrating functions to use `UniquePtr`, improving efficiency and safety. These refinements are crucial because they lead to better performance and reliability during audio and video calls, ensuring a smoother user experience. **Overall, these updates significantly enhance the way Firefox handles real-time communications.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) functionality in Firefox include the enhancement of its performance metrics and overall reliability. New features like RTCTransportStats, which track the amount of data sent and received during a call, and the improvement of logging practices for better debugging, ensure that developers have the tools they need to monitor and optimize video calls. Furthermore, the implementation of a more robust parser for H.264 video streaming will improve video quality by ensuring that requests and capabilities are better managed. This matters because **_better performance and reliability in video calls can significantly enhance user experience_**, making communications smoother and more efficient.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

features.summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser focused on improving memory management within session negotiation, specifically around the Session Description Protocol (SDP), which is crucial for setting up and managing communications in technologies like WebRTC (used for video calls). These changes involve using `UniquePtr`, a smart pointer that automatically manages memory, instead of traditional raw pointers, resulting in safer and more efficient code by preventing memory leaks and making ownership clearer. This transition helps maintain the stability and reliability of multimedia features where SDP plays a key role, ensuring a smoother experience when using features like video conferencing or streaming. **Overall, these changes enhance both performance and resilience, especially in communications-heavy applications.**

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alexandra Borovova
patches.subject: Invalidate device cache when recording a browser window on desktop in the privileged context.

patches.author: Andreas Pehrson
patches.subject: Add gtest.

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

features.summary: Recent updates in the camera and audio capture area of Firefox enhance how the browser manages device access during screen recordings, particularly when recording a browser window on desktop. Now, when recording, the device cache will be invalidated to ensure that only the most up-to-date information is used, which helps improve the reliability of capturing the correct audio and video sources. This change is significant for users who rely on features like WebRTC for video calls, as it minimizes potential issues with outdated device states, ensuring a smoother and more accurate experience. Users can expect **improved consistency** when using Firefox for multimedia tasks without noticing any latency or duplication in their recordings.

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.author: Nico Grunbaum
patches.subject: updated default_config_env for v150

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to check for missing branch-head commits.

patches.author: Michael Froman
patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build process included an adjustment to the **default configuration environment to version 150**, which helps ensure developers are using the latest supported features and optimizations for WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Additionally, two new Python scripts were added: one checks for missing branch-head commits, and the other displays existing branch-head commits for libwebrtc. These scripts enhance the development workflow, making it easier for contributors to keep track of changes and maintain up-to-date builds, thus facilitating smoother collaboration and reducing potential delays in the project’s progress.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Objc Video Capture

patches.author: serge-sans-paille
patches.subject: Comply with clang-tidy's modernize-use-nullptr check for dom/

features.summary: Recent updates to the video capture functionality in Firefox have focused on improving code quality by complying with modern coding standards. Specifically, the implementation has adopted the use of `nullptr` instead of traditional null values, following clang-tidy’s guidelines. This change enhances the robustness of the codebase, making it easier to maintain while ensuring compatibility with contemporary C++ development practices. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this type of improvement plays a vital role in maintaining a reliable and effective video capture experience.

patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.

features.label: Video Engine

patches.author: Alexandra Borovova
patches.subject: Allow capturing a browser in a priviliged context beyond Nighly.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox video engine have improved the ability to capture video from the browser in more secure or privileged contexts, extending this capability beyond just the Nightly version. This change is significant because it enhances the potential for applications that require video capture, such as screen recording tools or certain interactive web experiences. With this advancement, developers can more easily create features that utilize live video feeds directly from the browser, leading to richer user interactions and more innovative web applications. **This enhancement ultimately makes it easier for users to share and interact with video content securely.**

features.label: WebRTC tests

patches.author: Ryan Curran
patches.subject: Enable crashtest (opt) on macosx1500-aarch64-vms

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC tests have introduced crashtests for the MacOS platform running on Apple’s AArch64 architecture. This enhancement is important because it helps ensure that WebRTC — the technology behind video calls — functions reliably on the latest Mac hardware. **By enabling these crashtests, developers can proactively catch and address potential crashes, improving the overall stability and performance of WebRTC in Firefox.**
